{"query":"NHTSA investigation Tesla Model 3 fatal crash Texas autonomous vehicle rollout impact","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1628,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.cnbc.com/2026/06/22/tesla-nhtsa-model-3-crash-autopilot-katy-texas.html","title":"Tesla faces federal probe after Model 3 crash that killed 76-year-old","content":"The NHTSA is investigating a crash involving a Tesla Model 3 in which the car slammed into a Texas home and killed a 76-year-old.
The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said on Monday that it has opened a special crash investigation into a Tesla incident where a Model 3 slammed into a home in Katy, Texas, killing 76-year-old Martha Avila.
Harris County authorities said that the driver, Michael Butler, was cooperating with their investigation on the scene and said that he had been using Tesla's partially automated driving systems when his vehicle barreled out of its lane and into the home in the Houston suburb.
The federal vehicle safety regulator has opened more than three ​dozen ​Tesla ⁠special crash investigations involving the company's "advanced driver ​assistance systems," or partially automated driving systems, since 2016, when these became a part of the EV maker's new vehicles.
The latest NHTSA probe comes as Musk is trying to keep investors' faith that his automaker can become a global leader in autonomous vehicles."},{"url":"https://www.nbcdfw.com/news/national-international/tesla-federal-probe-deadly-model-3-crash-texas/4039904/","title":"NHTSA investigating Tesla after deadly crash into Texas home – NBC 5 Dallas-Fort Worth","content":"Jun 23, 2026 — Tesla faces federal probe after Model 3 slams into Texas home, killing a 76-year-old. Published June 23, 2026
Investigators say a Tesla was in self-driving mode when it crashed into a house in Katy, Texas, on Friday night.
The top U.S. auto regulator opened an investigation Monday after a Tesla that was reportedly using an automated driving feature slammed into a Texas home at high speed and killed a 76-year-old woman standing inside.
The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said it's opening a special investigation into the Tesla Model 3 crash on Friday near Houston, a significant probe because the car was allegedly using technology that Elon Musk considers key to the company's future.
It identified the woman killed as Martha Avila.
The feds are investigating Tesla’s "full self-driving" system after the company released reports on some of its crashes."},{"url":"https://www.forbes.com/sites/zacharyfolk/2026/06/22/tesla-driver-says-he-used-autopilot-in-fatal-crash---sparking-federal-probe/","title":"NHTSA Now Investigating Deadly Tesla Crash Into Texas Home","content":"The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration opened an investigation into a fatal crash last Friday, multiple outlets reported, days after a Tesla driver who was allegedly using his car’s Autopilot feature slammed into a woman’s house, killing a 76-year-old grandmother.
A driver in Texas claimed he was using his Tesla’s autopilot feature when it crashed into a home at a high speed.
The NHTSA also opened a special crash investigation into the collision, the Wall Street Journal first reported on Monday, as the case remains under investigation by the Harris County Sheriff's Office."}]}

{"query":"NHTSA investigation Tesla Model 3 fatal crash Texas autonomous vehicle rollout impact","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.nbcdfw.com/news/national-international/tesla-federal-probe-deadly-model-3-crash-texas/4039904","title":"NHTSA investigating Tesla after deadly crash into Texas home – NBC 5 Dallas-Fort Worth","content":"## The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said it's opening a special investigation because the car was allegedly using automated software that Tesla is rolling out on robotaxis this year. The top U.S. auto regulator opened an investigation Monday after a Tesla that was reportedly using an automated driving feature slammed into a Texas home at high speed and killed a 76-year-old woman standing inside. The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said it's opening a special investigation into the Tesla Model 3 crash on Friday near Houston, a significant probe because the car was allegedly using technology that Elon Musk considers key to the company's future. The Tesla CEO is rolling out robotaxis using automated software in several U.S. cities this year and plans to invite Tesla owners to put their cars into the fleet using the same system across the country. The driver told the Harris County Sheriff's Office that he was using the technology, according to a police report on the crash, but it's not clear what role, if any, it played in the incident.","score":0.8700796,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.cnbc.com/2026/06/22/tesla-nhtsa-model-3-crash-autopilot-katy-texas.html","title":"Tesla faces federal probe after Model 3 crash that killed 76-year-old","content":"The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said on Monday that it has opened a special crash investigation into a Tesla incident where a Model 3 slammed into a home in Katy, Texas, killing 76-year-old Martha Avila. Harris County authorities said that the driver, Michael Butler, was cooperating with their investigation on the scene and said that he had been using Tesla's partially automated driving systems when his vehicle barreled out of its lane and into the home in the Houston suburb. "FSD drives slowly through neighborhood streets and this was a high speed crash!" he wrote, referring to his company's partially automated driving systems, marketed as the premium, Full Self-Driving (Supervised) option today. Despite the implications of the brand name, Tesla warns drivers in their Model 3 owners' manual that, "Full Self-Driving (Supervised) requires you to pay attention to the road and be ready to take over at all times.". Tesla Vice President of Autopilot Ashok Elluswamy also chimed in on the incident with a response to Musk on X.","score":0.81595254,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=tRBYLfWICFU","title":"Fatal crash involving Tesla allegedly using driver assist now under federal investigation","content":"Fatal crash involving Tesla allegedly using driver assist now under federal investigation

A Tesla driver says he was using an “automated driving assistance system” when he crashed into a Texas home, killing a 76-year-old woman. The NHTSA is now investigating the incident. NBC News’ Aaron Gilchrist reports.
